name: Sunayana Samavedam
title: Researcher in Mechanistic Interpretability and Explainable Computer Vision
bio: I am a research scholar at the Center for Visual Information Technology (CVIT), IIIT Hyderabad, advised by Prof. P. J. Narayanan. My research focuses on mechanistic interpretability and explainable computer vision across vision-language models, multimodal reasoning, and robust representation learning. I have published work at ICVGIP 2025 (CAV Styler) and ICCV 2025 (PGBD), and I am currently working on sparse autoencoders under distribution shift and taxonomy-driven evaluation of spatiotemporal reasoning in VLMs.

publications.title: CAV Styler: Making Neural Style Transfer Interpretable and Controllable
publications.summary: A concept-driven neural style transfer framework that maps human-interpretable attributes like texture, blur, and detail into explicit controllable directions for fine-grained stylization.

publications.title: Prototype Guided Backdoor Defense via Activation Space Manipulation (PGBD)
publications.summary: A robust post-hoc defense against backdoor attacks using activation space geometry, including semantic trigger settings with a new celebrity face benchmark.

under_review.title: Evaluating Sparse Autoencoders for Mechanistic Interpretability under Distribution Shift
under_review.summary: We investigate how Sparse Autoencoder (SAE) feature quality degrades when CLIP ViT models are adapted to out-of-distribution domains (medical imaging, remote sensing) via parameter-efficient fine-tuning. We introduce a novel metric, Distribution-Aware Monosemanticity Score (DAMS), to characterize SAE feature quality under shifts that standard metrics often conflate. Experiments reveal a systematic tension between downstream classification accuracy and feature-level interpretability under distribution shift.

under_review.title: Toward a Taxonomy of Spatiotemporal Reasoning in Vision-Language Models
under_review.summary: We survey and systematically organize the landscape of spatiotemporal reasoning evaluation in vision-language models (VLMs), identifying critical gaps across five reasoning categories: perception, spatial relations, temporal, dynamic, and compositional. Building on mechanistic evidence that VLMs encode object locations via linearly bound spatial IDs, we argue that most benchmarks measure accuracy without probing internal representations, and that cognitively grounded tests from neuroscience (mental rotation, multiple object tracking, perspective-taking) remain largely unadapted for VLM evaluation. We propose a structured taxonomy mapping evaluated versus unexplored capabilities, highlighting compositional spatiotemporal reasoning and temporal causality as the most undercharacterized failure modes.

projects.title: CLIP-Based Domain Adaptation
projects.summary: Supervised and unsupervised domain generalization pipelines using CLIP for domain-agnostic feature learning across PCAS and DomainNet datasets.

projects.title: Spoofing of NIR-Based Facial Recognition Systems
projects.summary: Tested robustness of NIR-based 3D face recognition against high-fidelity 3D-printed spoofing attempts using ARTEC scanning and Blender texture mapping.

projects.title: 3D Object Reconstruction Using ICP SLAM
projects.summary: Built a rotating platform with Arduino for systematic object imaging, processed with COLMAP to generate precise 3D models from point clouds.

bullets: Led TAs for a 170+ student course; designed assignments on image filtering, feature detection, and enhancement.
Mentoring working professionals in deep learning through hands-on workshops on neural networks, computer vision, and frameworks.
Developed a POS-tagging based text classifier achieving 97% fiction/non-fiction accuracy; improved classification accuracy by 15%.
Built CNN models for image classification (92% accuracy); evaluated face anti-spoofing algorithms with zero-shot and one-shot learning.
Developed a CNN-based crack detection system for concrete structures achieving 96% accuracy under varied lighting.

news.text: Appointed WiDS Ambassador — organizing the WiDS Hyderabad Conference.
Attended CASML workshop on Physics-Informed Neural Networks and NVIDIA Modulus.
Two papers under review at CVIT, IIIT Hyderabad.
Served as Head TA for Digital Image Processing (170+ students).
